title: Future Voices Program 2026/27: Apply for Climate Change Journalism and Media Leadership Training in Southeast Asia
summary: Applications are now open for the Future Voices Program 2026/27 , a leadership and professional development initiative for early- to mid-career media professionals across Southeast Asia. This year’s program focuses on climate change journalism , providing participants with opportunities to strengthen their reporting skills, develop their leadership capacity, and connect with a regional network of emerging media professionals. The Future Voices Program is delivered by ABC International Development , also known as ABCID, with support from the ASEAN-Australia Centre . It is designed for media professionals who want to improve the quality, impact, and ethical foundations of climate reporting within their newsrooms and communities. A total of 33 media professionals will be selected for the 2026/27 cohort. Participants will take part in online learning, professional development activities over the course of a year, and continued engagement through the program’s alumni network. From the full cohort, 11 participants will later be selected to attend an intensive in-country program in Australia from 20 to 30 October 2026 . Applications must be submitted by 25 July 2026 . About the Future Voices Program The Future Voices Program is a regional media leadership initiative created to support emerging professionals working in journalism and the wider media sector across Southeast Asia. The program recognises that climate change is not only an environmental issue. It also affects economies, public health, migration, food security, energy systems, livelihoods, governance, and community resilience. Media professionals therefore play an important role in helping the public understand climate-related developments and the consequences they may have for different communities. Through the 2026/27 program, participants will be encouraged to strengthen their ability to report on climate change accurately, responsibly, and ethically. They will also develop leadership skills that can help them influence newsroom practices, guide colleagues, engage audiences, and contribute to stronger public-interest journalism. The program combines several forms of professional development, including: Online learning activities Leadership development Climate change journalism training Professional networking Ongoing alumni engagement Opportunities for selected participants to undertake advanced training in Australia Rather than focusing only on short-term training, Future Voices offers a longer professional development journey. Participants will remain involved in the program for approximately one year, allowing them to apply what they learn within their own professional environments. Future Voices Program 2026/27 Theme: Climate Change Journalism The central theme of the 2026/27 intake is climate change journalism . Climate reporting often requires journalists to explain complex scientific, economic, political, and social issues in language that is accessible to the public. It also requires careful verification, ethical judgment, and an understanding of how climate-related developments affect different groups of people. The Future Voices Program aims to strengthen climate reporting across both newsrooms and communities in Southeast Asia. Participants will have the opportunity to improve how they identify, investigate, and communicate climate stories. The program may be particularly relevant to media professionals whose work involves topics such as: Climate change and environmental policy Renewable energy and energy transitions Extreme weather and natural disasters Community resilience Sustainable development Agriculture and food security Climate-related displacement Public health and environmental risks Government and corporate climate commitments The social and economic effects of climate change Applicants do not necessarily need to work exclusively as climate journalists. The program is intended for emerging media professionals who are ready to strengthen their understanding of climate reporting and use that knowledge within their wider professional work. How Many Participants Will Be Selected? ABC International Development will select a cohort of 33 media professionals from across Southeast Asia. All 33 participants will join the broader Future Voices professional development program, which includes online learning, year-long development activities, and ongoing connections with the program’s alumni community. Of these 33 participants, 11 will be selected to attend an intensive in-country course in Australia. Being selected for the initial cohort does not automatically guarantee participation in the Australia-based component. The Australian intensive course is a further opportunity available to a smaller group chosen from among the 33 participants. Future Voices Program Structure The program has several interconnected components designed to support participants at different stages of their professional development. 1. Online Learning Participants will take part in online learning activities focused on climate journalism, leadership, and professional development. The online format will allow media professionals from different parts of Southeast Asia to participate while continuing their regular work. It will also create opportunities for participants to exchange experiences across different media systems, communities, and national contexts. 2. One Year of Professional Development The Future Voices Program is not limited to a single workshop or training session. Selected participants will receive approximately one year of professional development support. This extended structure gives participants time to reflect on the training, test new approaches, and apply their learning in their workplaces and communities. Participants may also have opportunities to build relationships with other media professionals facing similar reporting challenges across the region. 3. Regional Networking The program brings together emerging media leaders from Southeast Asia, creating a professional network that extends beyond individual newsrooms or countries. Participants will be able to connect with journalists and media professionals who bring different experiences, perspectives, and areas of expertise. This regional network may support future collaboration, knowledge-sharing, cross-border reporting, and continued professional exchange. 4. Alumni Engagement Ongoing alumni engagement is another important part of the Future Voices Program. Participants will not simply complete the program and lose contact with the network. Instead, they will become part of a wider alumni community that can provide continued opportunities for professional connection and regional engagement. This long-term alumni element reflects the program’s aim of developing a sustained community of emerging media leaders. Australia Intensive Course for 11 Selected Participants Eleven participants from the cohort will be selected to travel to Australia for an intensive in-country course from 20 to 30 October 2026 . The Australia component will provide additional academic, professional, and networking opportunities. During the intensive course, selected participants will: Earn a Leadership and Climate Change Micro-Credential Participants will complete a leadership and climate change micro-credential at a leading Australian university. A micro-credential is a focused academic or professional qualification covering a specialised area of knowledge. This component will allow participants to deepen their understanding of climate change while also developing practical leadership capabilities. Receive Training at the Australian Broadcasting Corporation Participants will receive professional training at the Australian Broadcasting Corporation , commonly known as the ABC. The training will expose participants to the practices and professional experience of Australia’s national public broadcaster. It may also help participants reflect on newsroom leadership, ethical journalism, public-interest reporting, and methods of communicating complex climate issues to broad audiences. Attend the All-Energy Australia Conference The selected participants will attend the All-Energy Australia conference . The conference brings together people working across the energy and climate sectors. Attendance will provide participants with an opportunity to learn about developments in renewable energy, energy policy, technology, and the broader transition toward lower-carbon systems. For journalists, exposure to climate and energy experts can support more informed reporting and help them identify potential sources, story ideas, and emerging regional issues. Connect With Australian Climate Institutions Participants will have opportunities to engage with major Australian climate institutions. These connections may help participants gain a broader understanding of climate research, policy, advocacy, and communication. They may also allow participants to compare Australian approaches with the climate challenges and policy debates taking place in Southeast Asia. Develop a Personalised Leadership Action Project Each selected participant will develop a personalised leadership action project with support from industry mentors. The action project will allow participants to translate the program’s training into a practical initiative connected to their own professional goals. Depending on the participant’s role and newsroom context, such a project could involve strengthening climate coverage, introducing new editorial practices, developing a reporting initiative, improving audience engagement, or supporting professional learning within a media organisation. The exact format of each project will be shaped by the participant’s professional responsibilities and development objectives. Who Should Apply? The Future Voices Program is intended for early- to mid-career media professionals in Southeast Asia . It is particularly suitable for applicants who are interested in developing their leadership abilities and improving the quality of climate journalism within their organisations or communities. Potential applicants may include professionals working in areas such as: Journalism News reporting Editing Broadcasting Digital media Radio Television Multimedia production Newsroom management Media development Public-interest communication The original announcement does not provide a complete list of accepted job titles. Applicants should therefore consider whether their current work clearly places them within the media profession and whether they can demonstrate how the program relates to their professional development. English-Language Requirement Applicants must be proficient in English. Because the program includes regional interaction, online learning, professional training, and possible participation in the Australia-based intensive course, participants will need sufficient English-language ability to communicate effectively and take part in all program activities. The announcement does not specify whether applicants must submit an English-language certificate or test result. Applicants should carefully review the official application form for any additional instructions concerning evidence of English proficiency. What Makes a Strong Applicant? Although the announcement does not provide detailed selection criteria, the program is likely to appeal most strongly to applicants who can demonstrate a clear connection between their professional experience and the aims of Future Voices. A strong application should clearly explain: The applicant’s current role in journalism or the media sector. Their experience as an early- or mid-career media professional. Their interest in climate change journalism. The leadership skills they hope to develop. How the program could improve their work or strengthen their newsroom. How they would use the knowledge gained to benefit their community or audience. Their willingness to participate in online learning and longer-term professional development. Their ability to communicate and participate in English. Applicants should avoid relying on general statements about being passionate or motivated. They should instead use concrete examples from their professional experience. For example, applicants could describe a climate-related story they have reported, an editorial challenge they have faced, a gap in climate coverage within their newsroom, or a leadership responsibility they would like to take on. Why Apply for the Future Voices Program? The Future Voices Program offers more than technical journalism training. It combines climate reporting, leadership development, regional networking, and long-term professional engagement. Successful applicants may benefit from: Greater knowledge of climate change and climate communication Improved reporting and editorial skills Stronger ethical journalism practices Leadership development Connections with media professionals across Southeast Asia Access to a continuing alumni network Opportunities to learn from Australian institutions Potential selection for the intensive course in Australia Academic recognition through a micro-credential for participants selected for the Australia component Mentorship while developing a leadership action project The program may be especially valuable for professionals seeking to take on greater responsibility within their newsrooms or to become stronger voices in climate journalism. Important Program Dates Applicants should note the following dates: Applications opened: 30 June 2026 Announcement updated: 1 July 2026 Application deadline: 25 July 2026 Australia intensive course: 20–30 October 2026 Program intake: 2026/27 Applicants should submit their applications before the deadline rather than waiting until the final day. Early preparation provides time to review written responses, confirm eligibility, and address any technical problems with the application platform. How to Apply Interested applicants must apply through the official Future Voices Program application link provided by ABC International Development. Before beginning the application, candidates should prepare information about their: Current professional role Media and journalism experience Leadership experience or potential Interest in climate change reporting Professional development goals Expected contribution to the regional network English-language ability Availability to participate in program activities Applicants should carefully review every section of the official application form because additional questions, document requirements, or selection conditions may appear there. APPLY HERE Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Future Voices Program 2026/27 is 25 July 2026 . Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered. Candidates are advised to confirm the deadline according to the time zone stated on the official application portal, where applicable. For more information about this opportunity, click here . Conclusion The Future Voices Program 2026/27 offers an important professional development opportunity for emerging media leaders across Southeast Asia who want to strengthen climate change journalism. With 33 participants joining a year-long program and 11 receiving the opportunity to attend an intensive course in Australia, Future Voices combines regional learning with advanced leadership training, institutional engagement, and professional networking. The program is particularly relevant at a time when communities need climate journalism that is accurate, ethical, accessible, and grounded in the realities of people affected by environmental change. Early- and mid-career media professionals who are proficient in English and committed to improving climate reporting should prepare and submit their applications before 25 July 2026 . Discover more global job opportunities, click here . The post Future Voices Program 2026/27: Apply for Climate Change Journalism and Media Leadership Training in Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: A Million Realities Fellowship 2027: Nominate Yourself or an Inspiring Youth Leader for a Two-Year Leadership Programme in the UK
summary: The A Million Realities (AMR) Fellowship 2027 is now accepting nominations for its next cohort of outstanding leaders and emerging leaders who are committed to improving the lives of young people in the United Kingdom. This prestigious two-year leadership programme brings together individuals from across the youth sector to collaborate, strengthen their leadership capacity, and drive meaningful change for young people transitioning into adulthood. Unlike many fellowship programmes that rely solely on direct applications, the AMR Fellowship begins with a nomination process. Whether you are nominated by someone who recognizes your leadership or choose to nominate yourself, this programme offers an exceptional opportunity to join a network of influential changemakers dedicated to creating a better future for young people. Nominations are open from July to September 2026 , with the successful Fellows beginning the programme in March 2027 . About the A Million Realities Fellowship The A Million Realities Fellowship is designed to identify and support leaders who are making a significant contribution to improving outcomes for young people across the UK. The Fellowship brings together individuals working in a wide range of sectors connected to youth development and wellbeing. Participants come from: Frontline youth organisations Education Public policy Mental health services Housing Philanthropy Community organisations Social enterprises Charities Youth advocacy initiatives Rather than focusing on the size or profile of an organisation, the Fellowship emphasizes the quality of leadership, demonstrated impact, commitment to young people, and the potential to influence positive change. The programme creates an environment where Fellows can strengthen their leadership abilities, exchange ideas, collaborate across sectors, and contribute to long-term improvements in youth development. Why the A Million Realities Fellowship Matters Young people face increasingly complex challenges as they transition into adulthood. These challenges may include access to education, employment, housing, mental health support, social inclusion, and economic opportunity. The A Million Realities Fellowship seeks leaders who are already working to address these issues and provides them with a platform to amplify their impact. By investing in leaders rather than projects alone, the Fellowship aims to strengthen the wider youth ecosystem and encourage collaboration between professionals from diverse backgrounds. Participants become part of a growing community of leaders committed to shaping policies, services, and initiatives that improve the lives of young people throughout the UK. Who Are the A Million Realities Fellows? A Million Realities Fellows represent a broad spectrum of professionals and emerging leaders whose work contributes to youth development. The Fellowship welcomes individuals working in areas such as: Youth development Education Community leadership Public policy Youth mental health Housing and homelessness Social justice Philanthropy Non-profit leadership Community engagement Youth empowerment Social innovation What unites Fellows is their commitment to creating opportunities for young people and their demonstrated ability to lead with purpose and impact. The Nomination Process One distinctive feature of the A Million Realities Fellowship is its nomination-based selection process. Why Nominations Matter A nomination serves as an independent endorsement of a candidate s leadership and influence. According to the Fellowship, a nomination is more than simply expressing interest—it acts as an important reference that demonstrates that others recognize the candidate s contribution and leadership within the youth sector. The Fellowship values nominations because they provide insight into a candidate s standing that may not be fully captured in a self-written application. Who Can Submit a Nomination? There are two ways to enter the Fellowship selection process: Nominate another leader Nominate yourself Although third-party nominations carry greater weight because they represent external recognition, self-nominated candidates have also successfully become A Million Realities Fellows in previous cohorts. Anyone wishing to nominate another person is encouraged to inform the nominee beforehand and obtain their permission before sharing personal details. Selection Timeline The Fellowship follows a structured selection process over several months. July – September 2026 Nominations open. During this stage, nominations are submitted for prospective Fellows. August – October 2026 Suitable nominees are invited to submit full Fellowship applications. Not every nominee automatically progresses to the application stage. The Fellowship team reviews nominations before inviting selected candidates to continue. 27–28 October 2026 Interviews take place. Candidates invited to interview will have an opportunity to discuss their leadership journey, impact, and aspirations. November 2026 – March 2027 Successful Fellows complete onboarding. During this period, selected participants prepare for the start of the Fellowship. March 2027 The two-year Fellowship officially begins. Fellowship Benefits Although the announcement primarily focuses on the nomination process, becoming an A Million Realities Fellow offers significant professional and leadership development opportunities. Participants can expect to benefit from: Membership in a prestigious UK leadership network Opportunities to collaborate with leaders across multiple sectors Enhanced leadership development Cross-sector networking Professional learning and knowledge exchange Greater visibility within the youth sector Opportunities to contribute to improving youth outcomes across the UK Participation in a two-year structured Fellowship programme Long-term connections with other Fellows and sector leaders The Fellowship is intended to strengthen participants capacity to create meaningful and lasting impact in their communities and organisations. Live Online Q A Sessions Prospective nominees, applicants, and nominators are invited to attend online information sessions where members of the AMR team and current Fellows will explain the programme and answer questions. The scheduled sessions are: 24 July 2026 Time: 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM 26 August 2026 Time: 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM These sessions provide an excellent opportunity to learn more about the Fellowship experience, eligibility expectations, and application process before submitting a nomination. Participants are encouraged to register in advance to receive joining details. Who Should Consider Applying? The Fellowship is ideal for individuals who: Demonstrate leadership within the youth sector Have a proven commitment to improving the lives of young people Are creating measurable impact in their communities Wish to strengthen their leadership capacity Value collaboration and cross-sector partnerships Are interested in joining a long-term network of changemakers The programme welcomes both established and emerging leaders whose work contributes to positive youth outcomes. How to Nominate a Leader To participate in the Fellowship selection process: Complete the official nomination form. Choose whether you are nominating yourself or another individual. If nominating someone else, obtain their consent before submitting their information. Wait for the Fellowship team to review nominations. Suitable nominees may receive an invitation to submit a full application. Contact Information Applicants or nominators who have questions about the Fellowship can contact the A Million Realities team via: Email: info@amillionrealities.com Why You Should Apply The A Million Realities Fellowship is an outstanding opportunity for leaders committed to improving opportunities for young people across the UK. Rather than simply recognizing past achievements, the programme invests in future leadership by bringing together talented professionals from different sectors to collaborate, learn from one another, and build stronger solutions for young people. Whether you work in education, mental health, housing, policy, philanthropy, youth development, or community leadership, the Fellowship provides an opportunity to expand your influence while becoming part of a powerful network dedicated to positive social change. If you—or someone you know—is making a meaningful difference in the lives of young people, this Fellowship offers a valuable platform to grow, collaborate, and lead. Click HERE to learn more and also apply for nomination. Conclusion The A Million Realities Fellowship 2027 seeks passionate leaders who are shaping better futures for young people throughout the United Kingdom. Beginning with a nomination process that recognizes leadership and community impact, the Fellowship offers participants the opportunity to join a two-year programme focused on leadership development, collaboration, and lasting change. Prospective candidates are encouraged to submit nominations before the close of the nomination period, attend one of the online Q A sessions to learn more about the programme, and prepare for the subsequent application and interview stages. For individuals dedicated to youth empowerment and systems change, the A Million Realities Fellowship represents an exceptional opportunity to strengthen their leadership journey while contributing to meaningful social impact. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE . The post A Million Realities Fellowship 2027: Nominate Yourself or an Inspiring Youth Leader for a Two-Year Leadership Programme in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Feminist Writers, Journalists, Researchers, Storytellers, and Creatives
summary: The Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship 2026 is now accepting applications from emerging and established feminist writers, journalists, researchers, storytellers, poets, creatives, and movement builders across Africa. This exciting fellowship provides participants with the opportunity to strengthen their writing skills, receive mentorship from experienced feminist scholars and practitioners, develop publication-ready work, and contribute to shaping conversations around African integration, feminism, and social justice. Under the theme Narrative Sovereignty, Knowledge Justice, and Feminist Futures, the fellowship seeks to empower African voices to tell authentic stories that challenge dominant narratives surrounding borders, migration, citizenship, identity, and belonging. It is designed for individuals who are passionate about using storytelling as a powerful tool for advocacy, social change, and the advancement of a more inclusive and borderless Africa. If you are committed to feminist storytelling, Pan-Africanism, knowledge production, and creating narratives that reflect African realities, this fellowship offers a unique platform to grow your skills while joining a vibrant community of changemakers across the continent. About the Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship 2026 Africa s vision of greater regional integration, free movement, and stronger people-to-people connections cannot be achieved through policy reforms alone. The stories people tell and the voices that are heard play a critical role in shaping public opinion, influencing policies, and redefining ideas of identity, citizenship, mobility, and belonging. The Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship recognizes that knowledge production and storytelling are powerful instruments for transforming societies. Through this fellowship, participants will critically examine whose stories are being told, whose knowledge is valued, and how feminist perspectives can contribute to building a more just, inclusive, and united Africa. The fellowship creates an environment where participants can explore complex social issues while producing compelling written work that reflects lived African experiences and feminist futures. Participants will engage in thoughtful discussions and writing projects that explore how narratives shape the African continent and influence conversations about mobility, migration, citizenship, gender equality, and regional integration. Key Questions the Fellowship Will Explore Throughout the fellowship, participants will critically engage with important questions such as: Whose knowledge is being produced and recognized? Whose narratives are amplified across Africa? Whose voices continue to remain unheard? How do stories influence public perceptions of borders, migration, identity, and belonging? What role can feminist storytelling play in promoting African integration? How can African storytellers imagine and articulate a truly Borderless Africa? How can feminist perspectives reshape conversations around justice, dignity, freedom, and inclusion? These discussions will help fellows produce thoughtful, impactful, and original written work that contributes meaningfully to ongoing debates across the continent. Benefits of the Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship Successful applicants will enjoy numerous professional and personal development opportunities throughout the fellowship. Participants will receive: 1. Two-Day In-Person Writing Retreat Fellows will participate in an immersive two-day writing retreat where they will collaborate with fellow writers, receive guidance from experienced facilitators, and refine their storytelling skills in an engaging learning environment. 2. Expert-Led Learning Sessions Participants will benefit from specialized training sessions covering important topics, including: Narrative sovereignty Feminist storytelling Knowledge justice Pan-Africanism Free movement across Africa Writing for advocacy Storytelling for social change These sessions are designed to deepen participants understanding of how writing can influence policy, society, and public discourse. 3. Professional Mentorship Each fellow will receive mentorship from experienced: Feminist scholars Professional writers Editors Pan-African practitioners This mentorship will support participants in strengthening their writing, expanding their perspectives, and producing high-quality work. 4. Editorial Support Participants will receive editorial guidance to help transform their drafts into polished, publication-ready articles, essays, creative works, or research pieces. 5. Publication Opportunities Outstanding works produced during the fellowship may be published through: Borderless Africa platforms Partner organizations Affiliated publication networks This offers fellows an opportunity to showcase their work to broader audiences across Africa and beyond. 6. Access to a Growing Pan-African Network Fellows will become part of a dynamic network of: Feminist writers Storytellers Researchers Advocates Pan-African practitioners Social justice leaders This network provides valuable opportunities for future collaboration, learning, and professional growth. Suggested Writing Areas Applicants are encouraged to propose writing ideas related to one or more of the following themes: Narrative sovereignty Feminist knowledge production Free movement and mobility Citizenship and belonging Cross-border identities Women and migration Border communities Pan-Africanism and regional integration Language, culture, and identity Digital storytelling and narrative change Feminist futures Speculative feminist writing Applicants may also propose other relevant ideas that align with the fellowship theme. Who Should Apply? The fellowship welcomes applications from individuals across Africa, including: Feminist writers Journalists Bloggers Researchers Poets Creative writers Community storytellers Human rights defenders Students Young activists Digital storytellers Content creators Movement builders Whether you are an emerging writer seeking mentorship or an experienced storyteller looking to deepen your engagement with feminist and Pan-African issues, this fellowship provides an excellent opportunity for learning and professional development. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should demonstrate: A strong interest in feminist issues and social justice. Commitment to African unity, mobility, citizenship, or Pan-Africanism. Passion for storytelling, writing, research, or advocacy. Willingness to fully participate in both the in-person writing retreat and online learning sessions. Ability to submit all required application documents. Importantly, no prior publication record is required , making this fellowship accessible to both emerging and experienced writers. Application Requirements Applicants are required to submit the following documents via email: campaigns@africans-rising.org A short biography (maximum 250 words). A statement of interest (maximum 500 words). One writing sample. Applicants are encouraged to ensure that all documents clearly demonstrate their interest in the fellowship theme and their commitment to feminist storytelling and social justice. Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare and submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute challenges. Why You Should Apply The Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship is more than a writing programme, it is a transformative learning experience that empowers African storytellers to shape the narratives that influence society. By participating, fellows will gain valuable mentorship, strengthen their writing abilities, connect with influential feminist thinkers, contribute to knowledge production rooted in African realities, and potentially publish work that reaches audiences across the continent. Whether your interest lies in journalism, creative writing, research, advocacy, or digital storytelling, this fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity to amplify your voice while contributing to conversations about justice, freedom, belonging, and the future of Africa. Learn more about Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship 2026: HERE Conclusion The Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for African writers, journalists, researchers, creatives, and advocates who are passionate about feminism, social justice, and Pan-Africanism. Through mentorship, expert-led training, editorial support, publication opportunities, and collaboration with fellow storytellers, participants will gain the skills and networks needed to create impactful narratives that challenge dominant perspectives and promote a more inclusive and borderless Africa. If you are ready to use your voice to influence conversations around identity, migration, citizenship, belonging, and feminist futures, this fellowship offers the ideal platform to develop your ideas and contribute to meaningful change. For more updates on Global FELLOWSHIPS , click HERE The post Borderless Africa Feminist Writing Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Feminist Writers, Journalists, Researchers, Storytellers, and Creatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ADB Innovation Challenges 2026: Win Up to USD 450,000 to Transform Laundry Systems and Urban Dengue Control Across Asia and the Pacific
summary: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) is inviting innovators, startups, companies, research institutions, NGOs, universities, and multidisciplinary teams from around the world to participate in two groundbreaking innovation challenges designed to tackle some of the most pressing development issues in Asia and the Pacific. The Laundry Transformation Challenge and the Urban Dengue Control Innovation Challenge are seeking scalable, affordable, and inclusive technological solutions capable of improving lives while creating measurable social, environmental, and economic impact. Selected innovators will have the opportunity to receive grant funding of up to USD 450,000 per pilot project , collaborate directly with ADB and government partners, validate their innovations in real-world environments, and gain international visibility. Whether your innovation addresses sustainable laundry technologies or next-generation dengue control, these challenges offer an exceptional opportunity to scale your solution while contributing to sustainable development across the region. About the ADB Innovation Challenges The Asian Development Bank (ADB) regularly launches innovation challenges aimed at identifying transformative technologies that address critical development problems throughout Asia and the Pacific. The 2026 challenges focus on two major issues: Transforming laundry systems to reduce women s unpaid work while protecting water resources. Developing bio-enabled and digital technologies to improve dengue control in Sri Lanka with potential expansion across Asia. Both initiatives emphasize innovation that is: Affordable Inclusive Environmentally sustainable Scalable Evidence-based Capable of generating measurable impact Successful applicants will pilot their technologies under real operating conditions with support from ADB and strategic local partners. Challenge 1: The Laundry Transformation Challenge Overview The Laundry Transformation Challenge seeks innovative technologies capable of transforming the way laundry is performed across Asia and the Pacific. Millions of households still rely on manual handwashing, particularly in lower-income communities where washing machines remain inaccessible or impractical. More than half of the population across Asia and the Pacific continues to wash clothes by hand, with women performing the overwhelming majority of laundry-related work. Laundry remains an invisible but enormous burden that consumes time, affects health, limits economic participation, and contributes significantly to environmental degradation. ADB is looking for solutions that can fundamentally rethink laundry systems while reducing water consumption, minimizing pollution, and improving livelihoods. Why This Challenge Matters Across Asia and the Pacific: Over 50% of people still wash clothes by hand. Women spend approximately 20% of their productive time performing laundry. Manual washing contributes to physical strain and repetitive injuries. Untreated greywater containing detergents and microfibers pollutes rivers and lakes. Growing urbanization is increasing pressure on already stressed water resources. Innovative laundry technologies could significantly improve gender equality, public health, environmental sustainability, and household productivity. The Challenge Question ADB asks innovators: How can innovative technology create more inclusive laundry systems that reduce women s unpaid workload while decreasing water consumption and pollution across Asia and the Pacific? Solutions may address one or multiple aspects of laundry transformation. Learn more and Apply Here Challenge 2: Urban Dengue Control Innovation Challenge Overview The Urban Dengue Control Innovation Challenge seeks innovative bio-enabled and digitally-powered technologies capable of transforming dengue prevention in Sri Lanka s rapidly growing urban areas. Despite years of investment in mosquito control, dengue remains a serious public health challenge. ADB is looking for technologies capable of generating strong evidence through pilot implementation, paving the way for city-wide and national expansion. Why This Challenge Matters Sri Lanka continues to experience dengue incidence rates of approximately: 300–400 reported cases per 100,000 people annually. This remains three to four times higher than the country s national target. Conventional mosquito control methods—including spraying, larviciding, and community clean-up campaigns—have struggled to provide sustainable results due to: Insecticide resistance Urban population growth Climate change Operational challenges Limited public health resources ADB believes a new generation of technology-enabled solutions can transform urban dengue management. The Challenge Question ADB asks innovators: How might bio-enabled and digitally-powered technologies deliver cost-effective, scalable urban dengue control solutions suitable for deployment throughout Sri Lanka and eventually across Asia and the Pacific? Learn more and Apply Here Who Should Apply? Both innovation challenges welcome multidisciplinary teams and organizations capable of developing scalable solutions. Potential applicants include: Startups Technology companies SMEs Universities Research institutions Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Engineering firms Health technology innovators Environmental innovators Social enterprises Innovation hubs Multidisciplinary teams Independent innovators Public-private partnerships Teams may consist of 1–10 members . Benefits of Participating Selected innovators will receive numerous advantages beyond financial support. These include: 1. Grant Funding Successful proposals may receive: Up to USD 450,000 per pilot project. Applicants should note that: A 10% co-financing contribution is required. 2. Pilot Your Innovation Participants will have the opportunity to: Test solutions under real-world conditions. Validate performance in new markets. Generate field evidence supporting larger deployment. 3. Collaborate with ADB Successful applicants will work alongside: Asian Development Bank experts National governments Local implementing partners International development stakeholders 4. Build Strategic Networks Participants gain exposure to: Government agencies Investors Development organizations Policy makers Industry experts 5. Increase Visibility Innovators will receive international recognition for their work among key stakeholders throughout Asia and the Pacific. 6. Drive Real Impact Selected projects have the potential to improve: Public health Women s economic empowerment Water conservation Environmental sustainability Urban resilience Community livelihoods Application Timeline Laundry Transformation Challenge Concept Note Submission 29 June – 31 August 2026 Full Application and Pilot Pitch 11 September – 23 October 2026 Winners Announced November 2026 Urban Dengue Control Innovation Challenge Concept Note Submission Deadline: 14 August 2026 Full Application and Pilot Pitch 7 September – 9 October 2026 Selected Technology Providers Announced November–December 2026 Pilot Implementation January 2027 Information Sessions Laundry Transformation Challenge ADB will host an online information session on: 24 July 2026 Time: 4:00 PM Manila Time (GMT+8) Applicants can attend to: Understand the challenge requirements Learn about evaluation criteria Ask questions directly to the challenge team Urban Dengue Control Innovation Challenge Applicants who missed the live webinar can watch the recorded information session featuring: Asian Development Bank National Dengue Control Unit of Sri Lanka The recording provides: Challenge overview Technology guidance Frequently asked questions Application insights Why You Should Apply Innovation has the power to solve some of society s biggest challenges. Whether you are developing sustainable household technologies or advanced public health solutions, these ADB Innovation Challenges provide an opportunity to transform your innovation into a real-world solution with regional impact. Participants will benefit from funding, expert collaboration, pilot implementation opportunities, and international recognition while contributing to healthier communities, gender equality, environmental sustainability, and stronger urban resilience. If your team has an innovative solution capable of creating lasting impact, this is an excellent opportunity to demonstrate its value on one of the world s leading development innovation platforms. Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post ADB Innovation Challenges 2026: Win Up to USD 450,000 to Transform Laundry Systems and Urban Dengue Control Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IBRO Parenthood Grants 2026: Funding for Early-Career Principal Investigators Worldwide
summary: The International Brain Research Organization is accepting applications for the IBRO Parenthood Grants 2026 , a funding program designed to help early-career principal investigators maintain continuity in their scientific research while approaching or experiencing parental leave. Pregnancy, childbirth, parental leave and adoption can occur during an important stage of a researcher’s professional career. During this period, researchers may face challenges in continuing experiments, producing research results, publishing papers and obtaining new grants. The IBRO Parenthood Grant seeks to address these challenges by providing financial support for research-related expenses. The program aims to help eligible researchers continue their scientific projects while managing pregnancy, recent childbirth, adoption or primary caregiving responsibilities. Applications for the second 2026 round opened on 1 July 2026 and will close on 15 August 2026 . Key Information About the IBRO Parenthood Grants 2026 Program name: IBRO Parenthood Grants Application status: Open Applications open: 1 July 2026 Application deadline: 15 August 2026 Open to: International applicants Maximum project duration: One year Maximum grant amount: Varies according to the applicant’s region Next application round: February 2027 Applications must be submitted through the IBRO Grants Portal using the application link available on the official opportunity page during the application period. About the IBRO Parenthood Grants Program Pregnancy, parental leave and adoption leave often coincide with a busy and important period in a researcher’s professional life. Researchers may experience difficulties in maintaining continuity in their work during this period. Such challenges can affect the production of research results, publication of scientific papers and ability to obtain further grant funding. A temporary reduction in research activity may also create the perception that a researcher has become less competitive, particularly when professional progress is measured through research outputs, publications and grants. The IBRO Parenthood Grant aims to counter this potential disadvantage by supporting early-career researchers who are approaching parental leave or experiencing circumstances related to pregnancy, childbirth or adoption. The funding can be used for approved research-related expenses that contribute to the continuation of the applicant’s scientific project. What Expenses Can Be Covered? The IBRO Parenthood Grant can be used to cover a range of research-related and professional expenses. According to the official program information, eligible expenses may include: Research reagents Research facility costs Outsourcing of experiments Data acquisition Transportation of non-commercial research samples Transportation of non-commercial reagents Travel expenses for attending a conference Childcare expenses related to conference attendance Grant-writing services Consultation services Applicants must provide a detailed budget breakdown identifying the items or services for which IBRO funding is requested. Who Can Apply for the IBRO Parenthood Grants? Applicants must meet the eligibility requirements stated in the official call. Early-Career Principal Investigator Requirement Applicants must be early-career principal investigators. They must have held the role of principal investigator for a maximum of seven years . Applicants are required to provide a signed and dated letter from their affiliated institution confirming their independent principal investigator status. Consideration of Previous Research Breaks Extended breaks from research may be accepted when determining an applicant’s eligibility. Such breaks may include previous maternity leave or adoption leave. Any relevant extended research break must be explained and justified in the application. Publication Requirement Applicants must have at least one published, peer-reviewed paper in which they have served at least as the corresponding author. Pregnancy, Birth or Adoption Requirement The applicant or the applicant’s partner must meet at least one of the following conditions: Be pregnant for at least four months; Have given birth within the four months before the grant application deadline; or Be close to adoption, with the applicant assuming the role of primary caregiver. Applicants must also provide an official letter confirming the relevant pregnancy, birth or adoption circumstances. Maximum Grant Amounts by Region The maximum grant amount that an applicant may request depends on the region in which the applicant resides. The maximum amounts specified by the respective IBRO Regional Committees are: Africa: USD 3,000 Asia-Pacific: USD 2,000 Latin America: USD 2,500 Pan-Europe: USD 4,000 United States and Canada: USD 3,000 Applicants should prepare their requested budget within the maximum amount applicable to their region. Project Duration The proposed project must be completed within a maximum period of one year from the proposed project start date. The research proposal submitted with the application should therefore describe the project for which IBRO support is requested and explain how the proposed work will be completed within the required period. Grant Reporting Requirement Successful applicants will be required to submit a grant report after completing the proposed project. The required report must be submitted no later than three months following the conclusion of the project . The remaining portion of the approved grant will be processed after the project has been completed and the required grant report has been submitted. Grant Payment Structure IBRO will transfer the approved funding according to an 80:20 payment arrangement . Under this arrangement: 80 percent of the approved grant will be awarded two months before the proposed project start date. The remaining 20 percent will be processed after the project has been completed and the required grant report has been submitted. Available Payment Account Options The grant funds may be transferred to: A bank account in the grantee’s name; An account belonging to the grantee’s institution; or An account belonging to another person directly involved with the grant, such as the applicant’s departmental chair. IBRO may consider exceptions depending on the individual circumstances. Information Required in the Application The IBRO Parenthood Grants application form asks applicants to provide the following information and supporting documents. 1. Applicant Details Applicants must provide a brief description of their academic background and current research focus. 2. Grants Received Applicants must provide a list of grants they have previously received, including any grants previously awarded by IBRO. 3. Research Proposal Applicants must submit a detailed description of the research project for which they are requesting IBRO support. 4. Motivation and Impact Statement Applicants must provide a comprehensive explanation of: Their motivation for applying for the program; and The impact that the requested funding will have on their research project and laboratory. 5. Budget Request Applicants must provide a detailed budget breakdown specifying the proposed items or services to be funded through the IBRO grant. 6. Curriculum Vitae A copy of the applicant’s current curriculum vitae must be submitted with the application. 7. Confirmation of Independent Principal Investigator Status Applicants must provide a signed and dated letter from their affiliated institution confirming that they hold an independent principal investigator role. 8. Confirmation of Pregnancy, Birth or Adoption Applicants must submit an official letter confirming the applicable pregnancy, birth or adoption circumstances. 9. Acceptance of Terms and Conditions Applicants must agree to the relevant terms and conditions, including the requirements stated in the IBRO Privacy Policy and payment guidelines. How Applications Will Be Evaluated Submitted applications will be assessed according to the evaluation criteria identified by IBRO. Eligibility Criteria The application will be assessed to determine whether all eligibility requirements stated in the call have been met. Scientific Credentials The applicant’s scientific credentials will be assessed based on: Academic record Publication record Relevant work experience Demonstrated progress to date Quality of the Research Proposal The proposed research project will be assessed according to whether it: Reflects a high standard of work; Addresses a significant research area; Contains clear aims and objectives; and Presents a structured plan. Applicant’s Justification The applicant must clearly explain the impact that the grant will have on their scientific progress. Funding Request The application must contain: A sufficiently detailed funding request; and A clear plan explaining how the requested funds will be spent. How to Apply for the IBRO Parenthood Grants 2026 Eligible applicants should follow the official application process outlined by IBRO. Step 1: Create an Applicant Account Applicants must create an account through the IBRO application system. Step 2: Review the Frequently Asked Questions Applicants may consult the official Frequently Asked Questions section for information about the grant and application process. Step 3: Prepare the Application Applicants must complete the required application sections and upload the necessary supporting documents, including: Applicant details List of grants received Research proposal Motivation and impact statement Detailed budget request Curriculum vitae Confirmation of independent principal investigator status Confirmation of pregnancy, birth or adoption Step 4: Access the Application Link The application link appears in the key information section at the top of the official program page during the application period. Applicants using the link will be redirected to the IBRO Grants Portal . Step 5: Submit the Application Before the Deadline The complete application must be submitted by 15 August 2026 . For technical issues with the application website, applicants may submit a customer-support request form. Application Rounds The IBRO Parenthood Grants program accepts applications twice each year. The current call is the second application round for 2026. The next call is expected to open in February 2027 . Applicants should refer to the relevant official call when the next application round opens. Contact Information Applicants with questions about the program may contact the IBRO programs team. The official IBRO Secretariat email address is: secretariat@ibro.org Visit HERE for more information. Conclusion The IBRO Parenthood Grants 2026 support early-career principal investigators who are managing pregnancy, recent childbirth, adoption or related primary caregiving responsibilities. The funding is intended to help eligible researchers maintain continuity in their scientific projects by covering approved expenses such as research reagents, facility costs, outsourced experiments, data acquisition, transportation of samples or reagents, conference travel, childcare related to conference attendance, grant-writing services and consultation services. Applicants must have held an independent principal investigator role for no more than seven years and must have at least one peer-reviewed publication as a corresponding author. They must also meet the applicable pregnancy, birth or adoption conditions and submit the required supporting documentation. The maximum award ranges from USD 2,000 to USD 4,000 , depending on the applicant’s region. Proposed projects must be completed within one year, and the final grant report must be submitted within three months after project completion. Applications opened on 1 July 2026 , and the final deadline to apply is 15 August 2026 . For more global opportunities, visit HERE . The post IBRO Parenthood Grants 2026: Funding for Early-Career Principal Investigators Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FAO Online UN Volunteers 2026: Join the International Year of the Woman Farmer Campaign as an Online Campaign Advocate
summary: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) is inviting passionate individuals to apply as Online UN Volunteers to support its advocacy campaign for the International Year of the Woman Farmer (IYWF) 2026 . This is the second call for applications , offering another opportunity for people around the world to contribute to a meaningful United Nations initiative without leaving their homes. The initiative is coordinated by the FAO Regional Office for the Near East and North Africa (RNE) and seeks dedicated Online Campaign Advocates who will help raise awareness about the vital role women play in agriculture and food systems. Through social media advocacy and digital engagement, selected volunteers will support FAO s efforts to amplify the voices of women farmers, promote gender equality, and encourage greater recognition of women s contributions to sustainable agriculture. This online volunteer opportunity is ideal for students, graduates, professionals, content creators, youth leaders, gender advocates, and anyone interested in using digital platforms to create positive social impact. About the International Year of the Woman Farmer (IYWF) 2026 Women play an essential role in global agrifood systems. They contribute significantly to food production, agricultural innovation, nutrition, environmental sustainability, and rural development. Despite their enormous contributions, many women farmers continue to face barriers such as limited access to land, financing, technology, education, markets, and decision-making opportunities. The International Year of the Woman Farmer (IYWF) 2026 aims to bring international attention to these challenges while celebrating the achievements and resilience of women working across agricultural value chains. The campaign seeks to: Increase public awareness about women farmers. Promote gender equality in agriculture. Highlight success stories from across the region. Encourage inclusive agricultural policies. Support sustainable food systems. Strengthen recognition of women s economic contributions. By joining this campaign, volunteers become part of a larger movement advocating for equal opportunities and greater visibility for women in agriculture. About the Online UN Volunteer Opportunity Selected volunteers will serve as Online Campaign Advocates , helping FAO extend the reach of its awareness campaign through digital platforms. Since the role is fully online, volunteers can participate from anywhere with an internet connection while contributing only a few hours each week. Volunteer Details Organization: Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) Programme: United Nations Online Volunteers Position: Online Campaign Advocate Volunteer Type: Online Time Commitment: 1–5 hours per week Duration: 12 weeks This flexible schedule allows volunteers to balance the opportunity with studies, work, or other personal commitments. Roles and Responsibilities As an Online Campaign Advocate, you will support FAO s regional communication efforts through digital engagement and awareness activities. Your responsibilities include: 1. Share Official FAO Campaign Content Volunteers will receive official campaign materials from FAO and publish them across their personal social media platforms to increase public awareness. 2. Promote Women s Contributions to Agriculture You will help educate your audience by sharing stories, facts, and campaign messages highlighting the important role women play in agrifood systems. 3. Raise Awareness About Gender Equality Advocates will encourage conversations around equal opportunities, inclusion, and the challenges women farmers continue to face. 4. Support Regional Advocacy Campaigns Throughout the International Year of the Woman Farmer, volunteers will help strengthen regional advocacy efforts by expanding the campaign s digital reach. 5. Monitor Campaign Engagement Volunteers will track engagement on their shared content and submit a short progress report every two weeks. Benefits of Becoming an Online UN Volunteer Participating in this FAO campaign offers numerous personal and professional benefits, including: Opportunity to support a United Nations initiative. Flexible online volunteering from anywhere. Gain practical experience in digital advocacy. Strengthen communication and social media skills. Expand your professional network. Contribute to gender equality and sustainable development. Support global awareness of women farmers. Enhance your CV with international volunteer experience. Build experience working with a UN agency. Make a meaningful contribution without relocating. Eligibility Requirements FAO is looking for enthusiastic individuals who are passionate about advocacy and digital communication. Applicants should have: Interest in gender equality. Interest in sustainable development. Interest in agriculture or agrifood systems. Experience using social media platforms such as: LinkedIn X (formerly Twitter) Instagram TikTok Strong communication skills. Responsible and professional online behavior. Working knowledge of English. Knowledge of Arabic or French is considered an added advantage but is not mandatory. Previous UN volunteering experience is not required. Why You Should Apply Digital advocacy has become one of the most effective tools for raising awareness about important global issues. Through this opportunity, volunteers can use their online presence to support meaningful conversations that promote inclusion, equality, and sustainable agriculture. Whether you have a large social media following or simply enjoy sharing informative content, your voice can contribute to amplifying the experiences of women farmers and inspiring positive change. The campaign also provides valuable exposure to international advocacy initiatives while allowing participants to gain practical experience working alongside one of the world s leading organizations dedicated to food security and sustainable agriculture. If you are passionate about women s empowerment, agriculture, climate resilience, rural development, or sustainable food systems, this volunteer role provides an excellent platform to make a real impact while building valuable professional skills. Application Deadline: No application deadline has been announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. Apply Here Discover more global volunteering opportunities on OFY here The post FAO Online UN Volunteers 2026: Join the International Year of the Woman Farmer Campaign as an Online Campaign Advocate appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: International Olympic Committee (IOC) Sustainability Trainee Internship 2026 in Lausanne, Switzerland: A Career-Building Opportunity for Recent Sustainability Graduates
summary: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has opened applications for its Sustainability Trainee Internship , offering an exciting 12-month professional opportunity for recent graduates who are passionate about sustainability, climate action, environmental protection, and using sport as a force for positive global change. Based in Lausanne, Switzerland , this full-time internship allows young professionals to gain practical international experience while contributing to one of the world s most influential sporting organizations. The IOC is globally recognized as the guardian of the Olympic Games and the leader of the Olympic Movement. Beyond organizing the world s biggest sporting event, the IOC works with governments, international organizations, sports federations, athletes, and commercial partners to advance sustainable development, inclusion, and positive social impact through sport. This internship presents a unique opportunity to work alongside experienced sustainability professionals and contribute to projects that influence the future of international sport and environmental responsibility. About the International Olympic Committee (IOC) The International Olympic Committee serves as the governing body of the Olympic Movement and is responsible for ensuring the successful organization and long-term development of the Olympic Games. The IOC collaborates with numerous international stakeholders, including: National Olympic Committees (NOCs) International Federations (IFs) Olympic Games Organising Committees Worldwide Olympic Partners Olympic broadcasting partners United Nations agencies International environmental organizations Governments and public institutions worldwide The organization believes that sport has the power to unite people, inspire communities, and contribute to a better and more sustainable future. About the Sustainability Trainee Internship The Sustainability Trainee will join the Olympic Games Department and work directly with the Sustainability Team. The successful candidate will assist in implementing sustainability initiatives that support the IOC s long-term environmental and social responsibility strategy. The internship is designed for recent graduates seeking practical international experience while developing professional skills in sustainability project management, stakeholder engagement, climate action, reporting, and environmental partnerships. Internship Details Department: Olympic Games Department Location: Lausanne, Switzerland Duration: 12 months Employment Type: Full-time Internship Activity Level: 100% Key Responsibilities As a Sustainability Trainee, you will contribute to a wide range of sustainability initiatives across the IOC. Your responsibilities will include: 1. Supporting Sustainability Projects You will assist the Sustainability Team in planning and implementing projects across several environmental focus areas, including: Climate action Climate adaptation Biodiversity conservation Sustainable procurement Circular economy initiatives 2. Supporting the IOC s Sustainability Strategy The trainee will contribute to sustainability initiatives across the IOC s three major areas of responsibility: As an international organization As the owner of the Olympic Games As the leader of the Olympic Movement 3. Working with International Partnerships The role involves supporting collaborations under internationally recognized sustainability initiatives such as: Sport for Climate Action Framework Sport for Nature Framework You will also engage with global partners committed to advancing sustainability in sport. 4. Organising Sustainability Events The trainee will help prepare and coordinate both internal and external sustainability events by: Preparing invitations Developing presentations Facilitating meetings Coordinating follow-up activities These events may involve: International Federations National Olympic Committees IOC commercial partners United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) 5. Preparing Reports and Dashboards The successful candidate will assist in producing reports that measure sustainability performance, including: IOC procurement sustainability reporting Carbon footprint monitoring Sustainability dashboards Progress tracking reports 6. Managing Sustainability Programmes The trainee will also coordinate selected sustainability programmes, including: IOC Climate Action Awards Athlete Sustainability Ambassador Programme 7. Supporting Cross-Departmental Projects From time to time, the trainee will provide support to projects involving multiple departments across the IOC. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following qualifications: Recently graduated with at least a Bachelor s degree in: Sustainability Sustainable Development Environmental Studies Or another closely related field Approximately one year of professional experience related to sustainable development Excellent command of English Knowledge of French or another language is considered an added advantage Strong proficiency in Microsoft 365 applications including: Word Excel PowerPoint Outlook Familiarity with sustainable development principles Understanding of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) is desirable Good knowledge of LinkedIn and professional social media platforms Personal Skills Required The IOC is looking for candidates who demonstrate excellent professional and interpersonal qualities. Ideal candidates should possess: Strong planning and organizational skills Excellent time management Dependability and accountability Strong written and verbal communication skills Critical thinking abilities Initiative and proactive problem-solving Teamwork and collaboration Respect for diversity and inclusion Ability to work effectively with people from different cultures and backgrounds Commitment to the Olympic values and ethical standards Why You Should Apply This internship provides much more than workplace experience. It offers the opportunity to become part of one of the world s most respected international organizations while contributing to meaningful sustainability initiatives. Benefits include: International work experience in Switzerland Professional development in sustainability Exposure to global environmental projects Networking with international organizations and experts Experience working alongside professionals from more than 70 nationalities Opportunity to contribute to climate action and sustainable sport initiatives Flexible working environment Competitive employee benefits Inclusive and diverse workplace Opportunity to support the Olympic Movement s sustainability goals This experience can significantly strengthen your career profile if you aspire to work in sustainability, international development, environmental policy, sports governance, climate action, or global organizations. Apply Here Learn more about this opportunity here Why This Opportunity Matters Sustainability has become one of the IOC s highest strategic priorities. Through this internship, successful candidates will gain first-hand experience contributing to environmental programmes that have worldwide influence across international sport. Working with organizations such as UNEP, IUCN, and UNFCCC provides invaluable exposure to international sustainability practices and strengthens future career opportunities in both public and private sectors. For graduates seeking a career where sustainability meets global impact, this internship offers a rare opportunity to help shape the future of responsible sport while building practical skills that are highly valued worldwide. Application Deadline: No application deadline has been specified. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY here The post International Olympic Committee (IOC) Sustainability Trainee Internship 2026 in Lausanne, Switzerland: A Career-Building Opportunity for Recent Sustainability Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Reimagining K-12 Assessments with AI: Renaissance Philanthropy and Google.org Launch Global Call for Innovative Ideas
summary: The future of K-12 education is evolving rapidly, and artificial intelligence is transforming how students learn, teachers teach, and schools measure academic progress. Recognizing this shift, Renaissance Philanthropy , with generous support from Google.org , has launched a global Request for Information (RFI) inviting innovators, researchers, educators, organizations, and technology experts to submit groundbreaking ideas on how advanced technologies can revolutionize K-12 assessments. The initiative aims to identify innovative approaches that can make assessments more continuous, actionable, and closely connected to classroom instruction. Rather than simply measuring what students know at a single point in time, Renaissance Philanthropy seeks ideas that leverage AI to provide richer insights into student learning, reasoning, and educational growth. Responses submitted through this open call will help shape future funding opportunities, including the prestigious Tools Competition , while also informing partnerships and broader efforts to strengthen education systems through innovation. Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 (11:59 PM Eastern Time) About the Call for Ideas Traditional assessment methods have remained largely unchanged for decades despite major advancements in educational technology. Most assessments continue to measure learning through isolated tests that capture only limited information about a student s abilities. Renaissance Philanthropy believes that artificial intelligence presents an unprecedented opportunity to rethink educational assessment entirely. Through this Request for Information, the organization hopes to gather innovative concepts that demonstrate how AI and emerging technologies can improve the way student learning is measured, understood, and supported. Rather than funding a single solution immediately, this initiative is designed to collect promising ideas from across the education ecosystem that may later receive support through future funding initiatives and collaborative partnerships. Why K-12 Assessments Need to Change According to Renaissance Philanthropy, today s assessment systems no longer reflect the realities of modern education. Current K-12 assessments often: Measure learning only during scheduled testing periods Capture only a limited portion of student knowledge and abilities Provide educators with insufficient information for instructional decision-making Fail to identify learning challenges until students have already fallen behind Focus primarily on final answers instead of the learning process itself As artificial intelligence becomes increasingly integrated into education, there is an opportunity to redesign assessments so they become dynamic tools that continuously support learning instead of simply evaluating performance. AI-powered assessment systems could provide deeper insights into: Student thinking processes Problem-solving strategies Higher-order reasoning Learning progression Areas where students need additional support Skills development over time Readiness for future learning This approach could enable educators to respond earlier and more effectively to student needs. Areas of Interest Renaissance Philanthropy and Google.org are particularly interested in innovative ideas addressing the following priority areas. 1. AI Integration and Assessment Innovation Applicants are encouraged to explore how artificial intelligence can significantly expand what educational assessments are capable of measuring. Potential ideas may include: AI-powered learning analytics Intelligent assessment platforms Adaptive assessment systems Continuous learning measurement Personalized feedback systems Automated insight generation for teachers The goal is to demonstrate how AI can make assessment more meaningful and actionable for educators and students alike. 2. Alignment with High-Quality Instructional Materials Another key focus is improving the connection between assessments and classroom instruction. Ideas may address: Curriculum-aligned assessments Assessment tools that integrate directly into teaching materials Better alignment between assessment results and classroom instruction Improved implementation of high-quality instructional resources This would help teachers better understand student progress within the context of what they are actually teaching. 3. Improving Assessment Quality and Design The organizers also seek ideas that improve the overall quality of educational assessments. Potential areas include: More valid assessment methods Fair and equitable assessment practices Accessible assessment tools Richer evidence of learning Better measurement of complex skills Authentic performance-based assessments Innovative assessment models should go beyond traditional multiple-choice testing to better reflect real-world learning. 4. Enhancing Student and Teacher Experience Assessment should support learning rather than create unnecessary burdens. Submissions may explore ways to make assessments: More engaging for students Easier for teachers to administer Less stressful More interactive More useful for classroom decision-making Better integrated into everyday learning Improving user experience can increase the value of assessments for everyone involved in education. 5. Connecting Assessment to Student Support Another priority is using assessment data to improve student support systems. Ideas may focus on: Early identification of learning challenges Intervention planning Progress monitoring Student support coordination Personalized learning pathways Data-informed educational decision-making Better assessment systems can help ensure that students receive timely assistance before learning gaps become more significant. How Artificial Intelligence Could Transform Assessment Artificial intelligence has the potential to fundamentally reshape educational assessment by moving beyond simple right-or-wrong scoring. Instead, AI can help educators understand: How students think through problems Where misconceptions occur Which learning strategies students use How reasoning develops over time What students are ready to learn next As AI tools become more common in classrooms, assessments must also evolve. Instead of focusing solely on final outputs, future assessments should capture the complete learning journey, including reasoning, collaboration, creativity, and problem-solving processes. This represents a major shift from periodic testing toward continuous learning insights. How Submitted Ideas Will Be Used Responses collected through this Request for Information will not only help Renaissance Philanthropy understand emerging opportunities in AI-enabled assessment but will also inform future investment decisions. Selected ideas may contribute to: Future funding opportunities The Renaissance Philanthropy Tools Competition New research collaborations Strategic partnerships Field-building initiatives Development of next-generation educational technologies The organization hopes these contributions will accelerate innovation across the broader education ecosystem. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare and submit their responses through the official submission platform before the application deadline. Applicants should ensure their ideas clearly explain: The educational challenge being addressed How advanced technologies or AI provide a solution The expected impact on K-12 assessment How the idea supports educators and students Opportunities for implementation and scalability Apply Now Important Deadline Applications must be submitted by 11:59 PM Eastern Time on 31 July 2026 . Late submissions may not be considered. Click HERE for Terms and Conditions For more questions, reach out to kristyn.manoukian@renphil.org . About Renaissance Philanthropy Renaissance Philanthropy is a nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing ambitious philanthropic initiatives in science, technology, and innovation.
