Founded by Tom Kalil and Kumar Garg—former senior advisors to U.S. Presidents Bill Clinton and Barack Obama—the organization works to identify breakthrough ideas, advise philanthropic investments, and incubate high-impact initiatives that address major public challenges. One of its flagship education initiatives is the Tools Competition, which supports innovative education technologies, research, and public goods capable of transforming learning at scale through collaboration with leading global funders. Conclusion The Renaissance Philanthropy and Google.org Request for Information represents a significant opportunity for innovators across the global education community to influence the future of K-12 assessment. As artificial intelligence continues to reshape education, this initiative seeks bold ideas that move beyond traditional testing models toward more continuous, insightful, and learner-centered assessment systems. Whether you are an educator, researcher, nonprofit leader, technology developer, startup founder, or education innovator, your ideas could help shape future funding opportunities and contribute to the next generation of educational innovation. With the 31 July 2026 deadline approaching, interested applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals and help redefine how student learning is understood, supported, and measured in the age of AI. For more Competition (Request for Information/Call for Ideas) opportunities, visit OFY HERE. The post Reimagining K-12 Assessments with AI: Renaissance Philanthropy and Google.org Launch Global Call for Innovative Ideas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Community Technology Fellowship 2026–2027 with the Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI)
summary: The Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI), a leading international nonprofit dedicated to making the online world safer for children and families, has announced an exciting opportunity for graduate students: the Community Technology Fellowship 2026–2027 . This fellowship is designed to empower young people with digital citizenship skills while equipping graduate students to become facilitators and community implementers of FOSI’s evidence-based online safety programs. Fellowship Overview Organization: Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI) Location: Hybrid – Remote In-person at community-based sites in Chicago, IL; Austin, TX; or Los Angeles, CA Position Type: Fellowship (Part-time) Grant Award: $10,000 total ($5,000 per semester) Program Dates: September 8, 2026 – May 2027 This fellowship provides graduate students with the opportunity to deliver FOSI’s Digital Citizens workshop series to youth in trusted community settings. Fellows will help young people build skills in AI literacy, social media wellbeing, healthy online relationships, digital boundaries, and responsible online behavior. Fellowship Goals The Community Technology Fellows Program is designed to: Develop youth digital citizenship skills through accessible, engaging workshops. Bring FOSI’s balanced online safety guidance into local institutions. Equip graduate student Fellows to serve as facilitators and community partners. Support community centers that lack staff capacity or curriculum for digital citizenship programming. Generate practical learning from youth and community partners to inform FOSI’s future resources and policy work. Key Responsibilities 1. Workshop Facilitation Youth Engagement Deliver FOSI’s Digital Citizens workshop series in assigned community centers. Facilitate interactive, discussion-based sessions for diverse youth groups. Create a welcoming, age-appropriate, and nonjudgmental learning environment. Guide youth through reflection activities, scenarios, and group discussions. 2. Community Center Implementation Support Serve as the primary Fellow contact for assigned sites. Coordinate scheduling and logistics with site liaisons. Support youth recruitment and engagement. Surface site-level needs and feedback to FOSI’s Programs Specialist. 3. Curriculum Delivery Program Materials Fellows will use standardized FOSI materials to deliver three core modules: AI, Chatbots You – Understanding how AI generates responses, evaluating information, and setting personal “AI rules.” Social Media Mental Health – Exploring attention design, mood impacts, identity, and healthy digital habits. Healthy Online Relationships Boundaries – Recognizing cyberbullying, distinguishing healthy vs. unhealthy behaviors, and building respectful communication standards. 4. Evaluation, Learning Reporting Track participation and engagement. Administer pre/post reflections or surveys. Collect informal feedback from youth and staff. Contribute recommendations for curriculum improvement. 5. Cohort Participation Professional Development Participate in Fellow training before program delivery. Join regular cohort meetings for coaching and shared learning. Collaborate with other Fellows to exchange strategies and insights. Represent FOSI professionally and uphold its balanced, evidence-based approach. Qualifications Required Current graduate student in a relevant field (education, public policy, social work, youth development, communications, public health, information studies, technology policy, psychology, sociology, etc.). Strong interest in youth digital citizenship, online safety, media literacy, AI literacy, or technology and society . Comfort facilitating workshops or discussions with youth. Excellent verbal communication and interpersonal skills. Strong organizational skills and ability to coordinate with partners. Ability to explain complex topics in clear, age-appropriate ways. Commitment to inclusive, culturally responsive programming. Reliability and professionalism in community-based settings. Preferred Experience working with teens in schools, youth programs, or community organizations. Experience facilitating workshops, trainings, or peer education sessions. Familiarity with digital citizenship, online safety, cyberbullying prevention, or media literacy. Experience collecting program feedback or survey data. Interest in nonprofit programming, community engagement, or public-interest technology. Time Commitment Part-time during the semester. Approximately two community center sites per semester . Includes training, workshop delivery, scheduling, participation tracking, and reporting. Flexible scheduling to accommodate graduate student commitments. Fellows will work 3 days a week (remote and in-person on community engagement days). A lunch stipend is included. Application Process Interested candidates should submit the following : A CV A cover letter describing interest and relevant experience A writing sample (policy or tech-focused preferred) Send your application to: fosi@fosi.org Deadline : Fellowship begins September 8, 2026 . Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis until a candidate is selected. About FOSI The Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI) is an international nonprofit organization working to make the online world safer for kids and families. FOSI convenes leaders from industry, government, academia, and nonprofits to collaborate on innovative solutions, research, and policy approaches. Through programs, events, and family-facing guidance, FOSI helps children, parents, caregivers, educators, and policymakers acknowledge risks, mitigate harms, and realize the rewards of digital life. Visit HERE to learn more about the Community Technology Fellowship 2026–2027 with the Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI) Conclusion The Community Technology Fellowship 2026–2027 offers graduate students a unique opportunity to combine academic interests with real-world impact. Fellows will play a vital role in empowering youth with digital citizenship skills, supporting community centers, and contributing to FOSI’s mission of online safety. With professional development, a generous stipend, and hands-on experience, this fellowship is ideal for graduate students passionate about technology, youth development, and social impact. To learn more about other fellowship opportunities, visit the OFY website HERE . The post Community Technology Fellowship 2026–2027 with the Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations University (UNU) Careers 2026: Apply for Three Exciting Global Opportunities in Research, Artificial Intelligence, Education, and Sustainable Development
summary: The United Nations University (UNU) Macau has announced applications for three outstanding international opportunities for researchers, educators, and professionals passionate about leveraging digital technologies to advance sustainable development. The available opportunities include the Visiting Research Fellowship (2026) , Training Educational Analyst , and Senior Researcher – Team Lead . These positions provide talented professionals and researchers with the opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge work in artificial intelligence, digital innovation, education, and sustainable development within the United Nations system. Whether you are an emerging researcher looking to collaborate on impactful projects, an education professional interested in designing innovative learning programmes, or an experienced research leader ready to shape global AI and digital policy, UNU offers a unique platform to make a global impact. About the United Nations University (UNU) The United Nations University is the academic and research arm of the United Nations. For more than five decades, UNU has generated policy-relevant research and educational initiatives addressing global challenges related to sustainable development, technology, governance, climate change, and human welfare. UNU Macau focuses on Digital Emerging Technologies for Sustainable Development (DET4SD) , exploring how artificial intelligence, digital innovation, and emerging technologies can support evidence-based policymaking and accelerate progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). 1. Visiting Research Fellowship (2026) The Visiting Research Fellowship provides researchers with the opportunity to work directly alongside UNU Macau experts on innovative projects exploring the intersection of digital technologies and sustainable development. The fellowship welcomes both early-career researchers and senior experts who can contribute innovative ideas and practical solutions that support governments and United Nations agencies. Fellowship Areas Research proposals may include topics such as: Artificial Intelligence Digital Governance Emerging Technologies Sustainable Development Public Policy Responsible AI Data-driven decision making Digital inclusion Expected Outputs Selected fellows will contribute through: Collaborative research Development of measurable applications Active engagement with UNU research teams Knowledge sharing Policy-oriented research outputs The fellowship typically lasts one to three months between September and December 2026 , with financial support provided to help offset travel and living costs in Macau. 2. Training Educational Analyst UNU Macau is also recruiting a Training Educational Analyst to strengthen its educational programmes and capacity-building initiatives. The successful candidate will support the design, delivery, and evaluation of innovative learning experiences while contributing to research and organizational development. Key Responsibilities Responsibilities include: Designing training programmes Facilitating learning activities Coordinating with stakeholders Evaluating learning effectiveness Preparing educational materials Supporting organizational development Conducting research aligned with institutional priorities Performing additional duties assigned by management This position is ideal for professionals passionate about education, instructional design, digital learning, and international capacity development. 3. Senior Researcher – Team Lead The Senior Researcher – Team Lead position is designed for experienced researchers capable of leading multidisciplinary research teams and delivering internationally recognized research. The successful candidate will lead research activities while building partnerships, supervising projects, mentoring researchers, and representing UNU in international academic and policy forums. Responsibilities Key responsibilities include: Leading high-quality research programmes Managing multidisciplinary research teams Building international partnerships Mobilizing research resources Publishing impactful research Presenting at international conferences Leading educational and training activities Supporting institutional strategy and innovation This role is well suited for accomplished academics and professionals with expertise in AI, emerging technologies, digital governance, and sustainable development. Why Apply? These UNU opportunities provide exceptional professional and academic benefits, including: Working within the United Nations system Contributing to global sustainable development initiatives Collaborating with leading international researchers Building expertise in artificial intelligence and digital innovation Expanding international professional networks Contributing to policy-relevant research Supporting governments and UN agencies with evidence-based solutions Application Deadlines Applicants should take note of the following deadlines: Visiting Research Fellowship: 24 July 2026 Training Educational Analyst: 2 August 2026 Senior Researcher – Team Lead: 9 August 2026 How to Apply Interested applicants should review the detailed requirements for their preferred opportunity and submit their applications through the official United Nations University Careers portal. Application links: Visiting Research Fellowship: Apply Here Training Educational Analyst: Apply Here Senior Researcher – Team Lead: Apply Here Conclusion The United Nations University Macau s latest recruitment presents an exciting opportunity for researchers, educators, and professionals eager to contribute to the future of artificial intelligence, digital transformation, and sustainable development. Whether you are seeking a research fellowship or a professional career within the United Nations system, these opportunities provide an excellent platform to work on globally significant projects while collaborating with world-class experts. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply before the respective deadlines. For more information on these opportunities, visit here. Discover more global opportunities on OFY, visit here.
